nodes.label: AI as independent verification tool
nodes.grounding_span: I will often run it through either ChatGPT or Claude to verify the information

nodes.label: AI for subjective decision support
nodes.grounding_span: Trauma registry is often very subjective. AI has been useful in helping me choose which applies

nodes.label: AI as respectful alternative to interrupting colleagues
nodes.label_negative: AI as unnecessary replacement for human expertise
nodes.grounding_span: I don't like interrupting others unless I really need to

nodes.label: AI reliance limited by nuance and subjectivity
nodes.grounding_span: There are too many nuances and subjectivity to rely on AI

nodes.label: Professional autonomy and independent validation
nodes.grounding_span: I feel more satisfied being able to confirm my selection with AI rather than needing to ask a coworker

nodes.label: Respect for colleagues' workflow
nodes.grounding_span: I don't like interrupting others unless I really need to

nodes.label: Accuracy in subjective medical documentation
nodes.grounding_span: Trauma registry is often very subjective

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for independent confirmation
nodes.grounding_span: I feel more satisfied being able to confirm my selection with AI

nodes.label: Skepticism about AI handling nuanced tasks
nodes.grounding_span: There are too many nuances and subjectivity to rely on AI

nodes.label: Forward-looking embrace of AI if it becomes part of role
nodes.grounding_span: If and when it becomes part of my position, I will embrace AI

nodes.label: Single-query consultation pattern
nodes.grounding_span: Its usually just a single question then I move on

nodes.label: Independent learning and self-reliance
nodes.grounding_span: Its something I do on my own
